result:
All spot-checks confirm the mappings are correct. This gives us 55 confirmed direct call sites (excluding comments, the `wake_up_process` definition itself, and `EXPORT_SYMBOL`).

## Results: calls to `wake_up_process()` under `kernel/` (55 total)

|---|---|---|

**Total: 55 direct call sites** across 34 files under `kernel/`.

Notes on exclusions:
- Excluded 14 occurrences that were comments/doc-references (e.g. `kernel/kthread.c:488,646,687,693`, `kernel/trace/trace_selftest.c:1236`, `kernel/locking/ww_mutex.h:328`, `kernel/time/timer.c:2111`, `kernel/time/hrtimer.c:2348,2382`, `kernel/bpf/cpumap.c:474`, `kernel/sched/core.c:981,999,1026`).
- Excluded the definition itself (`kernel/sched/core.c:4476: int wake_up_process(struct task_struct *p)`) and its `EXPORT_SYMBOL(wake_up_process)` at line 4480 â€” these are not calls.
- No other `wake_up_*` variants (e.g. `wake_up_process_no_notif`) matched the substring search, so nothing needed filtering on that front.
- The cbm graph's `CALLS` edges only resolve to file granularity (not per-function), so I used it solely to enumerate candidate files, then verified every call site and its enclosing function directly against the source with `grep`/`ctags`/manual inspection.
